By Matt Kramer

"How To Grow Your Brand Without Alienating Loyal Customers" <https://app.contentinstitute.com/e/er?s=1252280979&lid=6660&elqTrackId=BF7CC8E2B712B53DBB45B0B8A8684FEF&elq=8fb34487a9d94476b61d0f61c7b866de&elqaid=5266&elqat=1&utm_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&utm_medium=email&utm_source=eloqua&utm_campaign=WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&sp_eh=ecb16c8a410ecbc003dbe223e41bb0a79a5862b143585cc77e4c33778232d7e5&elqak=8AF5A31D426405521B0DB91ED6742A7A4E37CD8F61ADFE24F25101B3D67A20A71344>                                                               

Tilray Brands made consumer insights a cross-functional tool. See how quicker access <https://app.contentinstitute.com/e/er?s=1252280979&lid=6660&elqTrackId=128F7A320E9781B2BFB83DE404ABC89E&elq=8fb34487a9d94476b61d0f61c7b866de&elqaid=5266&elqat=1&utm_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&utm_medium=email&utm_source=eloqua&utm_campaign=WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&sp_eh=ecb16c8a410ecbc003dbe223e41bb0a79a5862b143585cc77e4c33778232d7e5&elqak=8AF5A1C54CBC0CAFEDB7870BAE7B73C765F1CD8F61ADFE24F25101B3D67A20A71344> to real-time data reshaped its retailer meetings, PR outreach, and brand innovation strategy.

READ OR LISTEN TO MORE STORIES FROM THIS WEEK

Why You Shouldn't Play 'Safe' With B2B Social Media <https://app.contentinstitute.com/e/er?s=1252280979&lid=6662&elqTrackId=E52C89C9DEBC4A58C874AED216BA535B&elq=8fb34487a9d94476b61d0f61c7b866de&elqaid=5266&elqat=1&utm_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&utm_medium=email&utm_source=eloqua&utm_campaign=WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&sp_eh=ecb16c8a410ecbc003dbe223e41bb0a79a5862b143585cc77e4c33778232d7e5&elqak=8AF5D40440DDB1D18CF14A6113A4550FCC32CD8F61ADFE24F25101B3D67A20A71344> with Amy Watts

Listen to the platform first and your B2B social media strategy is more likely to succeed. Expert Amy Watts covers what brands get wrong on TikTok and LinkedIn and digs into raw video, employee-generated content, and personal branding.

The Content Strategy Audit Hiding Inside Your AI Initiative <https://app.contentinstitute.com/e/er?s=1252280979&lid=6658&elqTrackId=6AD0346FDE4E80FF94D88195FC218E0F&elq=8fb34487a9d94476b61d0f61c7b866de&elqaid=5266&elqat=1&utm_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&utm_medium=email&utm_source=eloqua&utm_campaign=WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&sp_eh=ecb16c8a410ecbc003dbe223e41bb0a79a5862b143585cc77e4c33778232d7e5&elqak=8AF5CD0E307203BBE2C92A3B365059C72C53CD8F61ADFE24F25101B3D67A20A71344> by Robert Rose

Too many AI content initiatives sit on top of a content strategy problem. Follow this five-domain audit to surface what’s really broken — and how humans and AI can fix it before Q4 planning begins.

ICYMI: What Data Can't Tell You (But a Conversation Can) <https://app.contentinstitute.com/e/er?s=1252280979&lid=6603&elqTrackId=4EAE321B53AE2FD015AD444BA89E5819&elq=8fb34487a9d94476b61d0f61c7b866de&elqaid=5266&elqat=1&utm_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&utm_medium=email&utm_source=eloqua&utm_campaign=WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&sp_eh=ecb16c8a410ecbc003dbe223e41bb0a79a5862b143585cc77e4c33778232d7e5&elqak=8AF5D496C43CFB7032C9402FC9B1BF979241CD8F61ADFE24F25101B3D67A20A71344> by Clare McDermott

Candid insights are unlikely to come from surveys and AI answers. Small, in-person gatherings — advisory boards, executive dinners, and customer councils — generate fresh findings. Here’s what makes them work and how to run them well.

A NOTE FROM ROBERT ROSE

The AI content forecast: Cloudy with a chance of eyerolls

The company had a warehouse of really good content.

Three years of investment in resources, blog posts, and long-form articles. All well-researched, well-written, and strategically sound pieces that made up the kind of B2B content library most marketing teams would envy.

And they were getting nowhere with it.

A technology company in the financial services space, they were a challenger brand in a crowded market. Like many of their peers, they were repositioning as AI-forward. They came to me with what they thought was a scaling challenge — using generative AI to create more content for more channels.

But the real problem was deeper. They had a content strategy challenge, and the work we did ended up changing not just their AI implementation but their brand voice altogether. The relationship between their content, their ability to produce it, and their target audiences was the actual work.

Last week, I offered a reality check on the state of AI in content and marketing <https://app.contentinstitute.com/e/er?s=1252280979&lid=6599&elqTrackId=8E6E64439544BFB38E71C800FAE70178&elq=8fb34487a9d94476b61d0f61c7b866de&elqaid=5266&elqat=1&utm_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&utm_medium=email&utm_source=eloqua&utm_campaign=WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&sp_eh=ecb16c8a410ecbc003dbe223e41bb0a79a5862b143585cc77e4c33778232d7e5&elqak=8AF5C4D3D53197707858720600329AA14696CD8F61ADFE24F25101B3D67A20A71344>. The weather report: partly cloudy to stormy.

Why? Well, many companies bought into AI’s “efficiency miracle” promise. Now the lack of that promise’s fulfillment is generating a collective eyeroll.

And that means you may have an opportunity to move AI from the wrong jobs in your content strategy flow to the ones where it can actually help. 

What I’ve found useful in my consulting engagements is a practical five-domain strategy audit disguised as an AI integration assessment. The five domains to look at include:

*Strategy
*Capability
*Output
*Audience
*Measurement

This audit forces the modern content strategy conversation most teams have avoided for years.

And it starts with a single, meta question: “What are your goals for generative AI implementation in your content strategy? Why did you implement AI for content in the first place?”

If your answer is “to scale or speed up our content output,” you’re ripe for this audit. 

In the latest Rose-Colored Glasses <https://app.contentinstitute.com/e/er?s=1252280979&lid=6658&elqTrackId=B12F4F98C889926F8E74FC228ADF8AB3&elq=8fb34487a9d94476b61d0f61c7b866de&elqaid=5266&elqat=1&utm_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&_mc=em_WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&utm_medium=email&utm_source=eloqua&utm_campaign=WeeklyNews_6_12_26_AIForecast&sp_eh=ecb16c8a410ecbc003dbe223e41bb0a79a5862b143585cc77e4c33778232d7e5&elqak=8AF59B7A89988BC0FD8C1606CB26568193AFCD8F61ADFE24F25101B3D67A20A71344>, I walk you through the exact audit approach I took with one consulting client, including what to fix (and how) in each of the five domains. Let me know <mailto:robert@seventhbear.com?subject=CMI%20Content%20Feedback%20%E2%80%94%20marketing%20strategy%20audit%20AI&elqTrackId=064E9E6AF0233CB6089DD76892F03CE6> if this approach would help your team adjust for a successful second half of the year. 

Remember, it’s your story. Tell it well. 

"Robert Rose"                                                               

Robert Rose 

Chief Strategy Advisor 

Content Marketing Institute
